title: People task stayed unresolved until the human collapsed it to one concrete outcome
expected_state: The Task-0025 draft should already have said that the route title will be `People`, the primary CTA will be `Add person`, and the People surface will no longer show an onboarding button.
actual_state: The Task-0025 draft still used conditional wording such as `if an onboarding-style action remains...`, so the route title, primary CTA, and onboarding removal were still unresolved, and the producer was still handing off a branchy task shape where multiple People-surface outcomes were alive until the human corrected it.
human_costs: the task was too loose for the human to tell whether course correction was needed
the human had to finish the consensus work that should have happened before handoff

human_intervention.summary: The human rejected the unresolved task wording and supplied the concrete People-surface outcome that should have been in the task already.

feedback_quotes: That's a failure to build consensus; the product is still existing in a superposition of states.
That's concrete and something I can pick a side on.
I would say for any usability tasks we should collapse the waveform to a falsifiable or evaluat-able claim.

entries.target_state: The task should already state one concrete People-surface outcome: route title `People`, primary CTA `Add person`, and no onboarding button on that surface.
The task should be precise enough that the human can tell whether they agree or disagree with the proposed outcome before implementation starts.
The task should already state one concrete People-surface outcome: route title `People`, primary CTA `Add person`, and no onboarding button on that surface.
The task should be concrete enough that the human can pick a side on it instead of facing a superposition of plausible outcomes.
For usability tasks, the task should collapse to a falsifiable or evaluable claim about the expected user-facing resolution.
